name: La Berzosa y Hoyo de Manzanares — Sierra de Guadarrama
region_intro: Mountain biking around Hoyo De Manzanares offers diverse and technical terrain within the Community of Madrid, largely encompassed by the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park and Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The landscape is characterized by rocky sections, distinctive granite formations, and extensive holm oak forests. This area, often referred to as a natural bikepark, provides a variety of trails from flowing paths to challenging descents. Best mountain bike trails around Hoyo De Manzanares The most popular mountain bike trail is La Berzosa y Hoyo de Manzanares — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 13.8 miles (22.2 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied terrain of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is ¡Al Bucle y el Curveteo! — Sierra de Guadarrama, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This route offers a mix of technical sections and natural features within the Sierra. Local mountain bikers also love the La Berzosa Viewpoint – Covacho Waterfall loop from Parque puente de la Berzosa, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail leading through diverse forests and past the Covacho Waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Hoyo De Manzanares is defined by rocky and technical terrain, granite formations, and holm oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier flowing trails to advanced technical descents. The routes in Hoyo De Manzanares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Hoyo De Manzanares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Hoyo de Manzanares offers a good selection of mountain bike trails, with over 15 routes documented on komoot. These trails have been explored by more than 200 mountain bikers, highlighting the area's popularity for outdoor cycling.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its technical terrain, there are trails suitable for beginners. You can find easier, flowing paths that allow you to enjoy the landscape without overly challenging sections. For example, komoot lists 3 easy routes in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: The terrain in Hoyo de Manzanares is predominantly rocky and technical, featuring distinctive granite formations, natural drops, and jumps. Many trails follow ancient shepherd paths. The soil is generally hard and rarely muddy, but can become slippery in dry conditions, adding to the technical challenge.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Hoyo de Manzanares are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38233637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Berzosa Viewpoint – Covacho Waterfall loop from Parque puente de la Berzosa&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through diverse forests and past the Covacho Waterfall.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through diverse landscapes including extensive holm oak forests, juniper groves, and ash trees. You can encounter impressive granite formations and water features like the Covacho Waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38233637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Berzosa Viewpoint – Covacho Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience both the viewpoint and the waterfall. The region is also part of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park and Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there any viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints with panoramic vistas. The Mirador de La Berzosa is a notable spot, providing excellent views. You can incorporate it into your ride with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38233637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Berzosa Viewpoint – Covacho Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: The mountain bike trails in Hoyo de Manzanares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Riders often praise the varied and technical terrain, the beautiful natural surroundings, and the challenging descents that make it a renowned Enduro spot.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can find historical points of interest. The archaeological site of La Cabilda offers a glimpse into the area's past. Additionally, nearby attractions include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored before or after your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Hoyo de Manzanares?
A: Hoyo de Manzanares can be enjoyed year-round. The hard soil rarely gets muddy, making it suitable even after rain. However, be aware that dry conditions can make some technical sections slippery. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Hoyo de Manzanares is known as a natural bikepark with trails that cater to advanced riders. Routes like 'Lechuza' are famous for their steep sections, loose terrain, and drops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38233637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Berzosa Viewpoint – Covacho Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is also rated as difficult, offering a significant challenge with nearly 500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I reach the mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Hoyo de Manzanares is located just 35 kilometers from Madrid, making it accessible. While specific public transport options directly to trailheads might vary, the town itself is connected, and from there, you can often access the trail network. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the mountain biking areas?
A: The town of Hoyo de Manzanares offers various amenities, including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. These establishments provide a convenient stop for refreshments and meals.
tips: Mixed technique descent with rock and dirt path with deep grooves

The Mirador de La Berzosa is a natural panoramic viewpoint located in the town of La Berzosa, near Hoyo de Manzanares, in the Community of Madrid. It's an ideal spot for hiking, landscape photography, and nature lovers.

The stream that comes down from Peña Herrera only has water in the rainy season, the rest of the year it is dry. When there is abundant water, a very peculiar waterfall forms between the granite bowls. The transit of the current continues its journey over granite slabs towards La Berzosa. In the rainy season it usually has many visitors.
Located north of the La Berzosa urbanization, this viewpoint points to the natural area with the same name, as well as the city of Madrid.
Located north of Hoyo de Manzanares, from it you can see Berzosa and the city of Madrid.

During the 1960s, the Sierra de Hoyo temporarily disguised itself as the Wild West. Movies like "A Fistful of Dollars", "The Good, the Bad and the Ugly" or "Death Had a Price", were filmed in this place, Golden City, where there were previously sets typical of spaghetti westerns, starring the now legendary Clint Eastwood.
However, the policies to protect the Madrid environment that were created years after the cinema town was abandoned led to the removal of the sets, leaving only the location with its views of the Sierra de Hoyo, and an augmented reality recreation of the place. .
